news.headline: Oracle stock has worst week since 2001 dot-com bust as AI financing concerns escalate

news.headline: Walmart heir Lukas Walton buys minority stake in the Chicago Bulls and United Center
news.summary: Lukas Walton is the 39-year-old grandson of Walmart founder Sam Walton. He and Samantha Walton are residents of Chicago.

news.headline: After Issuing Its First Stock Split in 2020, Tesla Took Just 2 Years to Issue Its Second Split. Could a Third Stock Split Come in 2026?
news.summary: Both of Tesla's previous stock splits happened during the month of August -- but don't expect another one to happen two months from now.

news.headline: S&P 500, Nasdaq End Week Lower As Investors Rotate Out Of Tech, AI Plays — ON, AAPL, SLS, INFQ, NKE in Focus
news.summary: The Dow Jones ended its third consecutive week higher amid cooling oil prices and strength in pharma and materials stocks.

gamePlan: Bias into tomorrow: Mixed — favor defined-risk spreads.
Use QQQ $706.52 as the risk-on/off line; trade with it, not against it.
Primary idea: LLY CALL — enter $43.65–$48.25, target $68.93+, stop $27.57.
Keep total options exposure modest; one A+ setup beats five marginal ones.
report.narrative: Stocks closed lower with SPY -0.72% and QQQ -1.38%. Composite sentiment -14 (Mixed). The tape is being driven mainly by qqq / mega-cap tech, spy trend, sector rotation. VIX at 15.31 (+0.62%). Environment read: favor defined-risk spreads. Leadership came from Healthcare; Energy lagged. Today's standouts: LLY +7.13%, MSFT +5.71%. Laggards: AVGO -3.67%, AMD -2.06%. Carrying LLY as the top idea into tomorrow (6.4/10).
report.riskWarning: Options trading is risky and can result in the loss of the entire premium paid. This report provides analysis and educational information only — not financial advice. Size positions small (1–2% of capital per idea), honor stops, and do your own research.
